content:
One of Angular’s Greatest Features Is Also One of Its Most Misunderstood

4 min read
1 day ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Ask Angular developers what makes Angular different from many frontend frameworks.
A common answer is:
Dependency Injection.
It’s one of Angular’s most powerful capabilities.
It allows applications to be modular, testable, and flexible.
But over the years I’ve noticed an interesting misconception.
Many teams believe that if they’re using Dependency Injection correctly, they automatically have a good architecture.
Unfortunately, that’s not how architecture works.
Dependency Injection Solves One Problem
Dependency Injection answers a very specific question:
How does an object receive the things it depends on?
Instead of creating dependencies directly:
const api = new CustomerApiService();Angular injects them:
constructor(
private customerApi: CustomerApiService
) {}
